title: salix novae-angliae myrtillifolia
intro: Salix novae-angliae myrtillifolia, also known as the New England willow or gray willow, is a type of shrub or small tree belonging to the willow family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, derived from the salicylic acid present in its bark, which is similar to aspirin. The medicinal properties of Salix novae-angliae myrtillifolia offer several health benefits, including the relief of pain, reduction of fever, and treatment of rheumatoid arthritis. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, and can be found in various forms in health food stores and online retailers.
